seoTitle: Build a Custom Email Template in HubSpot
metaDescription: Design a reusable HubSpot email template with the drag-and-drop builder, branding elements, personalization tokens, and cross-client testing.
intro: Building a reusable email template in HubSpot means setting up a consistent layout once and reusing it for every future campaign. This guide covers starting a new template from the Design Manager, arranging structural blocks, and adding your branding and personalization tokens. It ends with saving the layout and testing how it renders across devices.

steps.heading: Open the Templates directory
steps.body: Go to Marketing, then find the Files and Templates directory in HubSpot.

steps.heading: Create a new template
steps.body: Click Create Template and select the drag-and-drop email framework.

steps.heading: Name and categorize it
steps.body: Input an identifiable template name and assign a category label for organization.

steps.heading: Arrange your layout
steps.body: Use the sidebar module panel to drag structural sections into the email canvas.

steps.heading: Add branding elements
steps.body: Insert your logo, set typography, and define background color rules for consistency.

steps.heading: Personalize and save
steps.body: Inject personalization tokens into text blocks, add a footer module, and save your completed layout.

faq.question: Can I reuse a HubSpot email template across campaigns?
faq.answer: Yes, once saved, custom templates are available in your library for future email campaigns.

faq.question: Do I need design skills to build a template?
faq.answer: The drag-and-drop editor is built for non-designers, letting you arrange blocks without writing code.

faq.question: Can I add personalization tokens to a template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can inject dynamic tokens into rich-text blocks to personalize emails sent from the template.

faq.question: Should I test the template before using it live?
faq.answer: Yes, running a test render across multiple device previews helps catch formatting issues before sending.
